title: Customers cannot login to the customer portal after morning maintenance
description: Starting at 07:30 UTC, customer-portal users report HTTP 500 after entering credentials. SSO login redirects loop back to the IdP. Internal employee portal using same IdP is unaffected. Maintenance window ended at 07:25 UTC and included a renewal of TLS certificates on the SSO endpoint.
logs_excerpt: auth-service ERROR: idp_callback handshake_failure: x509: certificate signed by unknown authority\nauth-service WARN: jwks fetch from https://idp.acme.example/.well-known/jwks.json returned 502\ngateway 502 upstream connect error: TLS handshake timeout

title: Order database extremely slow, checkout transactions timing out
description: Orders service throws transaction timeout errors. p95 query latency jumped from 40ms to 4500ms at 14:00 UTC. No deployment in last 24h. A weekly bulk-pricing job is currently running on the orders.line_items table.
logs_excerpt: orders ERROR: deadlock detected; victim transaction rolled back (PID 8471)\npg WARNING: process 8471 still waiting for ShareLock on transaction 998123\norders ERROR: canceling statement due to lock_timeout

title: Deployment failed, payment-service pods crashlooping
description: Helm release v3.42.0 of payment-service triggered a rollout. New pods reach Running briefly then OOMKilled. Previous version v3.41.7 was stable. The release notes mention a new fraud-scoring library bundled in the image.
logs_excerpt: kubelet: Container payment-service exited with code 137\npayment-service: java.lang.OutOfMemoryError: Java heap space\nkubelet: Back-off restarting failed container

title: Disk usage 95% on prod node-3, alerts firing
description: Filesystem /var on node-3 at 95%. Mostly /var/log and container overlay layers. Other nodes at 60-70%. Node-3 hosts the logging aggregator pod after a recent reschedule.
logs_excerpt: kubelet: Disk pressure: True\nfluentd: buffer overflow, dropping records\nsystemd-journald: Journal file rotated due to size limit

title: External payment provider API calls timing out intermittently
description: Calls to api.processor.example time out roughly 1 in 5 requests. Started after CoreDNS pod was restarted this morning. Internal service-to-service traffic is healthy. Hardcoded IP fallback test succeeds reliably.
logs_excerpt: payment-service ERROR: dial tcp: lookup api.processor.example on 10.96.0.10:53: read udp i/o timeout\ncoredns: plugin/errors: forward .: i/o timeout 8.8.8.8:53

title: Memory usage grows unbounded on recommendation-service
description: Resident memory of recommendation-service climbs ~80MB/hour and pods get OOMKilled every ~36 hours. Started after we enabled an in-memory feature cache for personalization. No corresponding eviction logic was added.
logs_excerpt: recommendation-service: heap usage 7.8GB / 8GB\nkubelet: OOMKilled\nprometheus: container_memory_working_set_bytes monotonically increasing

title: Daily backup job failed with checksum mismatch
description: Nightly logical backup of warehouse-db completed but post-restore verification on the staging restore target failed checksum. Previous 14 nightly backups verified successfully. Underlying storage reports no errors.
logs_excerpt: backup-job: pg_dump completed in 38m\nverify-job: ERROR sha256 mismatch on table warehouse.events\ns3: GetObject succeeded, ETag matches manifest

title: Web frontend returns 504 intermittently from EU region
description: EU users report intermittent 504 Gateway Timeout. NA region unaffected. EU edge load balancer forwards to a regional API gateway whose p99 latency tripled in the last 4 hours. CPU on backend pods is normal.
logs_excerpt: edge-lb 504 upstream timeout after 30000ms\napi-gw WARN: connection pool exhausted, queue depth 120\nbackend: GET /catalog 200 in 28000ms
